document.title: Usability Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector documenting intended use, participants, test methodology, task results, conclusion, and reference standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports usability validation by documenting participant feedback, satisfaction ratings, task completion rates, ease or difficulty ratings, time on task, errors, and recommendations for improvements.
keyPoints: The ADD is described as a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, not for mammography.
Seven participants were involved in the usability test.
Task completion success rate criteria were 100% for each usability task, and the reported result was 100% pass.
The report concludes that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.
Referenced standards include EN ISO 14971:2007, EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015, and EN 62366:2008.

concepts.name: Usability validation
concepts.evidence: A usability test is intended to determine the extent an interface facilitates a user’s ability to complete routine tasks.

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: This device (ADD) intended use is as follows:

concepts.name: Task completion success rate
concepts.evidence: Task Completion Success Rate - Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. - Result: 100 % (Pass)

concepts.name: Residual risk regarding usability
concepts.evidence: residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable

evidence.claim: The document is a Usability Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The file number is A-TCF-5-040 and the report is Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: File No A-TCF-5-040 Rev. No 0; Report No.: A-TCF-5-040 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: ADD is intended to obtain diagnostic information through anatomical structure of the human body using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR, or DR.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient through the anatomical structure of human body obtained by a film or an image processing (workstation) process of examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR (Portable flat panel).

evidence.claim: ADD is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and child human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Seven participants were involved in the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants were involved in a usability test to ensure stable results.

evidence.claim: Participants included persons educated about the medical device and licensed equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.
evidence.evidenceText: Person who receive education about medical device and to have a licence equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.

evidence.claim: The task completion result was 100% pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Task Completion Success Rate - Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. - Result: 100 % (Pass)

evidence.claim: The evaluation tasks included user manual usability, power cord connection, cable connection, main power button action, PC software settings, battery charging/change, patient arrangement, indicators, and cleaning.
evidence.evidenceText: ACT #1) Usability of User manual; ACT #2) Connecting the power cord; ACT #3) Cable code connection; ACT #4) Main power button action; ACT #5) PC software settings; ACT #6) Battery charging, change; ACT #7) Arrange the patient; ACT #8) Indicators; ACT #9) Cleaning

evidence.claim: The report concludes usability residual risk is minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

evidence.claim: Re-evaluation of usability will be performed if new use-error issues are raised.
evidence.evidenceText: The re-evaluation of usability will be performed once new use-error issues are raised.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text.
The source path and metadata suggest CE MDD context and model HAD1417MCW, but the document text itself only identifies ADD/Abyz Digital Detector.
Section 5 states the summative evaluation was performed according to a colon, but the referenced plan or criteria after the colon are missing in the extracted text.
Some source wording appears grammatically degraded, but the main metadata and conclusions are readable.
